title: Share of cancer deaths attributed to risk factors
subtitle: The estimated share of deaths from [cancers](#dod:cancer) attributed to all [risk factors](#dod:risk-factor). The remaining share represents deaths that would be expected to have occurred in the absence of these known risk factors.
caption:
Share of cancer deaths attributed to risk factors

The estimated share of deaths from cancers attributed to all risk factors. The remaining share represents deaths that would be expected to have occurred in the absence of these known risk factors.

This horizontal bar chart displays the share of cancer deaths attributed to risk factors for various cancer types worldwide in 2023. The data is sorted in descending order, with Cervical cancer at the top showing a value of 100%, indicating that all deaths from this cancer type are attributed to risk factors. Mesothelioma follows closely with 91.7%. A cluster of respiratory and upper digestive tract cancers occupies the next tier: Lung, tracheal and bronchus cancers stand at 76.4%, while Larynx cancer is at 73.4%. Moving down the list, Esophageal cancer is at 56.4%, Colon and rectum cancer at 56.2%, and Liver cancer at 48.2%. The middle section of the chart shows values ranging from roughly 27% to 40%, including Lip and oral cavity (39.4%), Pancreatic (38.7%), Uterine (33.3%), Bladder (33%), Breast (29.8%), and Kidney (27.9%). Lower down, Nasopharynx cancer is at 23.7%, Stomach at 19%, and Leukemia at 18.7%. The bottom portion of the chart features Thyroid cancer at 11.6%, Gallbladder and biliary tract at 11.4%, Multiple myeloma at 7.6%, and Non-hodgkin lymphoma at 4.8%. Prostate cancer has the lowest attributed share at 0.9%. The note accompanying the data mentions that the methodology can include negative values in some cases to reflect potential protective effects or uncertainty, though no negative values are visible in this specific selection of cancer types.
